data.title: Grifols approves IPO of its US biopharma business
data.description:
Spanish drugmaker Grifols said on Tuesday it has approved a U.S. initial public offering of its U.S. biopharma business.

Grifols, which makes drugs using blood plasma, plans to float a minority stake in the biopharma business. The parent company will maintain its listing in Spain and retain majority ownership of the U.S. business.

"The transaction reinforces Grifols' vision of self-sufficiency by creating the first and only player that will not rely on plasma, manufacturing or supply from outside the United States," Grifols said.

The sale of stake through the IPO would raise capital to strengthen its balance sheet, cut debt and support investments in key markets, it added.

Grifols said in February it expects core earnings to grow by more than a quarter in 2026 and will continue to cut debt, after reporting 2025 profit that more than doubled.

The group will continue to grow its plasma and diagnostics businesses in other key markets, it added on Tuesday.

data.title: Australia equities poised to rise at open, NZ up
data.description:
Australian shares were set to open higher on Wednesday led by likely gains in miners as commodity prices strengthened, while investors await February inflation data later in the day.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) rose 0.6%, an 88.6-point premium to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark rose 0.2% on Tuesday.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.3% in early trade.

data.title: Arxis, Mission-Critical Components Manufacturer, Files for Nasdaq IPO
data.description:
Arxis filed an S-1 to go public on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker "ARXS." The company plans to sell Class A common stock, with final pricing, size, and number of shares to be determined. Proceeds are expected to repay borrowings under its credit facilities and fund general corporate purposes, while sponsor Arcline will retain voting control through high-vote shares.

Business Description

Arxis designs and manufactures proprietary, mission‑critical electronic and mechanical components engineered to perform in extreme environments. Its products include interconnect solutions, RF and microwave components, power products, sensors, precision and self‑lubricating bearings, seals, springs, gaskets, ducting, and radar‑absorbing materials. The company sells through 47 customer‑facing brands and is built on 67 foundational proprietary technologies, collaborating engineer‑to‑engineer with customers to design in components early in platform lifecycles.

Arxis operates two segments—Electronic Components and Mechanical Components—and serves defense & space, commercial aerospace, and specialized industrial markets. In 2025, about 47% of revenue came from defense & space, 23% from commercial aerospace, and 30% from industrial technology. Its “designed‑in” positions on more than 600 platforms, a diversified base of 5,000+ customers, and a decentralized operating model supported by its Arxis EDGE data-driven system underpin recurring revenue and long platform durations.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Broad exposure across defense & space, commercial aerospace, and industrial technology with multi-decade platform cycles.
  • Market growth: Global aircraft deliveries projected to rise ~74% from 2024 to 2028; commercial fleet expected to double by 2042.
  • Market position: Embedded supplier on 600+ platforms with over 5,000 customers and 67 foundational proprietary technologies.
  • Key competitors: Fragmented supplier landscape ranging from large corporate divisions to small private specialists; no single dominant peer disclosed.
  • Industry trends: Rising defense budgets (NATO spend up from ~$236B in 2015 to ~$484B in 2024; U.S. defense budget projected to reach ~$1T by FY2026), modernization programs, and increasing demand for high-performance and miniaturized components.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: 5,000+ with top 10 accounting for 36% of 2025 revenue; no customer >7%
  • Locations: 72 specialized manufacturing facilities, including 55 in the U.S.
  • Geographic presence: Global footprint with ITAR-compliant U.S. sites
  • Partnerships: 47 customer-facing brands and deep engineer-to-engineer collaboration with OEMs and Tier 1/2 suppliers
  • Other key metrics: Embedded on 600+ platforms; 67 foundational proprietary technologies; >500 engineers and >500 technical sales reps; 11,000+ “layer cake” revenue layers; ~5,750 employees (2025)

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): $1,591.0 million (2025)
  • Revenue growth: 114.1% YoY in 2025 (8.9% organic); 10.5% pro forma CAGR (2021–2025)
  • Gross profit: $754.7 million (47.4% margin)
  • Operating income: $276.7 million
  • Net income: $46.0 million (2025)

Management

  • Kevin Perhamus, President and Chief Executive Officer - Former CEO of Arxis predecessors Quantic and Qnnect; prior leadership roles at Winchester Interconnect, Teradyne, and Amphenol.
  • Azad Badakhsh, Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer - Former Managing Director at Moelis & Company with extensive M&A and capital markets experience.
  • Jennifer Allen, Chief Legal Officer and Secretary - Former CAO, SVP, General Counsel and Secretary at Triumph Group; prior senior roles at CIRCOR and BAE Systems.
  • Jason Roth, President, Electronic Components - Previously led Pacific Aerospace and Electronics; leadership roles at multiple TransDigm companies.
  • Ross Sealfon, President, Mechanical Components - Former President & CEO of Quantic; prior leadership at Winchester Interconnect.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: Arxis, Inc.
  • Filing date: March 24, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: ARXS
  • Exchange: Nasdaq Global Select Market
  • Lead underwriters: Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC; Morgan Stanley; Jefferies (joint bookrunners), with Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets among others
  • Use of proceeds: Repay borrowings under revolving and term credit facilities, with the remainder for working capital, general corporate purposes, and potential acquisitions.

Original SEC Filing: Arxis, Inc. - S-1 - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: MaxCyte Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• MaxCyte Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 6 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -10 cents. The mean expectation of six analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 8 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -9 cents to -7 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 16% to $7.30 million from a year ago; analysts expected $9.41 million.

  • MaxCyte Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 9 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $9.6 million.

  • MaxCyte Inc shares had fallen by 51.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the advanced medical equipment & technology peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for MaxCyte Inc is $5.00, about 85.2% above its last closing price of $0.74

data.title: Australia Stock Futures Pointing to Early Advance — Market Talk
data.description: Australian stock futures are pointing to an opening advance despite a soft lead from U.S. equities and a rebound in oil prices. ASX futures are up by 0.6% ahead of Wednesday's session, suggesting that the S&P/ASX 200 will add to Tuesday's 0.2% rise. The benchmark index faded after rising by as much as 1.7% in early trade, but still snapped a three-day losing streak that had pushed it to a 10-month low. Ahead of the open, Rio Tinto said it had reached a A$2 billion deal with lawmakers to keep open its Boyne aluminum smelter in eastern Australia. In the U.S., the DJIA lost 0.2%, the S&P 500 slipped 0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite gave up 0.8%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: RAC Group said to be sounding out investors for London IPO
data.description:
According to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter, the driving services firm has been holding early look meetings with fund managers in recent weeks, as it seeks to lay the groundwork for a possible market debut this year.

In February, sources told Bloomberg that a deal could value RAC, which is backed by private equity groups CVC Capital Partners and Silver Lake, at about £5bn.

Sources told Bloomberg on Tuesday that no final decisions have been made and details of the offering including timing could change.

The RAC provides breakdown assistance and motor insurance, among other things.

data.title: Australia unveils $1.4 billion support for Rio's Boyne aluminium smelter
data.description:
Australia on Wednesday unveiled A$2 billion ($1.40 billion) over 10 years to support mining major Rio Tinto's Boyne Smelters, the country's second-largest aluminium smelter, to stay open.

The investment, equally funded by the federal and Queensland state governments, will unlock almost A$7.5 billion in new investment in Queensland, with Rio Tinto underwriting investment in energy assets, Federal Industry Minister Tim Ayres said.

Rio Tinto said, in a separate announcement, that the initiative would help position the smelter to run on solar and wind power, as the Anglo-Australian miner looks to reduce emissions and maintain competitiveness amid rising fossil fuel costs.

The deal ensures Boyne Smelters Limited, in which Rio Tinto holds a majority stake, will continue aluminium production beyond the expiry of its existing power contract in 2029 and through to at least 2040.

Australia's metals processing sector has been under pressure from high energy and labour costs, and governments announced financial support for several facilities last year.

Rio Tinto has also agreed to take 40% of output from Lightsource bp's Lower Wonga solar and battery project, equivalent to 112 megawatts of solar capacity, taking its total contracted renewable power capacity in Queensland to more than 2.8 gigawatts.

($1 = 1.4296 Australian dollars)

data.title: Reed’s Reports Q4 Revenue $7.5M, FY Sales $34.1M; Leadership Change with Interim CEO Appointed
data.description:
Reed’s reported fourth-quarter 2025 net sales of $7.5 million and a full-year 2025 revenue of $34.1 million, with gross profit of $1.5 million in Q4 and $7.0 million for the year. The company narrowed its net loss to $3.8 million in Q4 (versus $4.1 million a year earlier) but reported a full-year net loss of $15.8 million. Reed’s also announced a leadership transition, naming Neal Cohane as interim chief executive officer effective March 24, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Net sales of $7.5 million in Q4 2025; $34.1 million for FY 2025 (comparatives: $9.7M Q4 2024; $38.0M FY 2024).
  • Gross profit and margin: Q4 gross profit $1.5 million (20% margin) versus $2.9 million (30%) in Q4 2024; FY gross profit $7.0 million (20% margin) versus $11.4 million (30%) in FY 2024.
  • Operating income (loss): Loss from operations of $(3.5) million in Q4 2025 and $(15.1) million for FY 2025 (comparative Q4 2024 loss $(3.7)M; FY 2024 loss $(8.1)M).
  • Net loss: Q4 net loss of $(3.8) million (loss per share $(0.44)); FY net loss of $(15.8) million (loss per share $(1.91)).
  • EBITDA (non-GAAP): Q4 EBITDA $(3.6) million; FY EBITDA $(14.6) million.

Business Highlights

  • Leadership transition: Neal Cohane appointed interim CEO (effective March 24, 2026) after former CEO Cyril Wallace stepped down; board has initiated search for permanent CEO.
  • Management focus: Company emphasizing profitable growth via operational discipline, in-house sales complemented by outsourced brokers, expansion of direct store distribution, brand marketing, product innovation, and Asia expansion.
  • Operational improvements: Sequential quarterly improvement reported in net sales, gross margin, and net loss during Q4 driven by increased retail velocity and seasonal product offerings with select natural, specialty, grocery, mass, and e-commerce customers.
  • Cost and efficiency actions: Delivery and handling costs reduced to $2.46 per case in Q4 2025 from $3.00 per case year-over-year; SG&A reduced to $4.0 million in Q4 2025 from $4.9 million a year earlier.
  • Corporate actions: Recent uplisting to the NYSE American exchange noted as a step to broaden investor engagement; board member Rudolf J.M. Bakker to serve as outsourced fractional CMO following CMO departure.

Original SEC Filing: REED'S, INC. [ REED ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Oak Woods Acquisition Received Notice of Delisting
data.description:
On March 23, 2026, Nasdaq notified the company that, due to not completing a business combination within 36 months as required by Listing Rule IM-5101-2, its securities will be delisted. Trading will be suspended at the open on March 25, 2026, and Nasdaq is expected to file a Form 25-NSE; the company may seek OTC quotation and aims to complete a business combination and re-list in the future.

data.title: Cannabist Launches CCAA Restructuring, Sells Delaware and Ohio Assets for $63.5 Million
data.description:
Cannabist announced multiple agreements tied to a court-supervised restructuring, including sales of its Delaware and Ohio operations totaling $63.5 million. In Delaware, Parma Holdco will acquire assets for $16.5 million, with escrowed amounts for indemnities and adjustments and a $3.3 million deposit due shortly after signing. In Ohio, Holistic Industries will purchase all equity of Cannabist’s subsidiaries for $47 million, comprising $34.5 million at closing and a $12.5 million note, plus an option and funding framework for an additional location. Cannabist also secured a Support Agreement with noteholders representing about 60% of its secured notes to back the sale program and CCAA process, subject to regulatory, court, and noteholder approvals.

Agreement 1: Cannabist to Sell Delaware Assets to Parma Holdco for $16.5 Million With Escrow Protections

  • Agreement type: Asset purchase agreement for Delaware assets
  • Counterparty: Parma Holdco; Millstreet Credit Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing
  • Reason: Raise liquidity and exit Delaware operations

Agreement 2: Cannabist Sells Ohio Operations to Holistic Industries in $47 Million Cash-and-Note Deal

  • Agreement type: Equity purchase agreement for Ohio subsidiaries
  • Counterparty: Holistic Industries
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing
  • Reason: Monetize Ohio assets to support restructuring

Agreement 3: Cannabist Secures Noteholder Support Agreement to Back Asset Sales and Canadian CCAA Process

  • Agreement type: Support Agreement for restructuring and asset sales
  • Counterparty: Supporting Noteholders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Obtain creditor backing for restructuring and sales

Original SEC Filing: Cannabist Co Holdings Inc. [ CBSTF ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Amphenol Enters Underwriting Agreement for €500 Million 3.625% Senior Notes Due 2031
data.description:
Amphenol, through subsidiary Amphenol Technologies Holding, entered an underwriting agreement to issue €500 million of 3.625% senior notes due 2031, guaranteed by Amphenol. The underwriter group includes Barclays, Citigroup Global Markets Europe, Commerzbank, HSBC, BofA Securities Europe, Goldman Sachs, TD Global Finance, U.S. Bancorp Investments, Loop Capital Markets, and Siebert Williams Shank. Closing is expected on Mar 30, 2026, subject to customary conditions. The transaction is intended to raise long-term funding and support corporate flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Underwriting agreement for €500 million 3.625% senior notes due 2031
  • Counterparty: Barclays; Citigroup Global Markets Europe; Commerzbank; HSBC; BofA Securities Europe; Goldman Sachs; TD Global Finance; U.S. Bancorp Investments; Loop Capital Markets; Siebert Williams Shank
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing (expected Mar 30 2026)
  • Reason: Raise long-term funding and enhance financial flexibility

Original SEC Filing: AMPHENOL CORP /DE/ [ APH ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: Fifth District Bancorp 2025 10-K: $22.058M Interest Income, $0.80 EPS
data.description:
Fifth District Bancorp reported a strong turnaround in its 2025 results, driven by higher yields and larger loan and investment balances that lifted total interest and dividend income to $22.058 million and produced net income of $4.088 million. Improved net interest margin drove net interest income (reported as gross profit) to $12.848 million, and diluted and basic earnings per share rose to $0.80. Results were also aided by a $3.5 million gain on insurance proceeds and other one-time gains that contributed to income before taxes of $4.185 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $22.058 million, up $2.8 million (14.4%) versus 2024 due to higher yields and larger investment and loan balances.
  • Gross Profit (reported as Net Interest Income): $12.848 million, up $2.792 million versus 2024 reflecting improved net interest margin.
  • Income (Loss) Before Income Taxes: $4.185 million, a turnaround from a $(1.436) million loss in 2024 driven by higher interest income and one-time gains.
  • Net Income: $4.088 million, versus a $(1.078) million loss in 2024; benefited from a $3.5 million gain on insurance proceeds and higher net interest income.
  • Income (Loss) per Share - Basic and Diluted: $0.80, improved from $(0.21) in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth & Margin Improvement: Net interest income rose 27.8% year-over-year driven by higher loan yields and reinvestment in higher-yielding securities.
  • Channel & Product Mix Shift: Continued focus on fixed-rate 1-4 family mortgages to retain portfolio while moderately growing commercial lending and participations.
  • Deposit & Funding Trends: Ongoing efforts to grow core deposits with a modest shift from money market accounts to certificates of deposit and NOW accounts to support liquidity.
  • Operational Momentum & Service Model: Emphasis on community-oriented, full-service loan servicing and conservative underwriting to sustain asset quality.
  • Capital & Liquidity Posture: Maintained strong liquidity and elevated capital levels to support organic growth and opportunistic branching and acquisitions.

Original SEC Filing: Fifth District Bancorp, Inc. [ FDSB ] - 10-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
